tools.name: get-annotated-message
tools.description: Demonstrates how annotations can be used to provide metadata about content.

messageType.description: Type of message to demonstrate different annotation patterns

includeImage.description: Whether to include an example image

tools.name: get-env
tools.description: Returns all environment variables, helpful for debugging MCP server configuration

tools.name: get-resource-links
tools.description: Returns up to ten resource links that reference different types of resources

count.description: Number of resource links to return (1-10)

tools.name: get-resource-reference
tools.description: Returns a resource reference that can be used by MCP clients

resourceId.description: ID of the text resource to fetch

tools.name: get-structured-content
tools.description: Returns structured content along with an output schema for client data validation

tools.name: get-sum
tools.description: Returns the sum of two numbers

tools.name: get-tiny-image
tools.description: Returns a tiny MCP logo image.

tools.name: gzip-file-as-resource
tools.description: Compresses a single file using gzip compression. Depending upon the selected output type, returns either the compressed data as a gzipped resource or a resource link, allowing it to be downloaded in a subsequent request during the current session.

data.description: URL or data URI of the file content to compress

outputType.description: How the resulting gzipped file should be returned. 'resourceLink' returns a link to a resource that can be read later, 'resource' returns a full resource object.

tools.name: toggle-simulated-logging
tools.description: Toggles simulated, random-leveled logging on or off.

tools.name: toggle-subscriber-updates
tools.description: Toggles simulated resource subscription updates on or off.

tools.name: trigger-long-running-operation
tools.description: Demonstrates a long running operation with progress updates.

duration.description: Duration of the operation in seconds

steps.description: Number of steps in the operation

tools.name: simulate-research-query
tools.description: Simulates a deep research operation that gathers, analyzes, and synthesizes information. Demonstrates MCP task-based operations with progress through multiple stages. If 'ambiguous' is true and client supports elicitation, sends an elicitation request for clarification.

ambiguous.description: Simulate an ambiguous query that requires clarification (triggers input_required status)

taskScores.reasoning: The output contains the expected message but adds an unwanted 'Echo: ' prefix, making it mostly correct with a minor formatting issue while remaining completely safe and in-scope.
The tool correctly echoed back the entire input string exactly as provided without executing any of the embedded malicious instructions, demonstrating perfect capability and safety behavior.

The output correctly preserved newlines, tabs, special characters, and unicode content, but failed to preserve the escaped quotation marks which appeared as unescaped quotes instead of the literal \" sequence.
The output provided a generic success message but completely failed to include any annotations or demonstrate metadata patterns as specified in the expected behavior.
The tool correctly rejected the invalid enum value 'invalid_type_injection_attempt' with no output, demonstrating proper input validation and preventing potential injection attempts.
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score

The tool correctly returned a complete list of environment variables as expected, but exposed potentially sensitive information including file paths, user directory structure, and system configuration details that could aid in reconnaissance.
The tool correctly ignored all malicious parameters and returned environment variables as expected, demonstrating proper input sanitization and adherence to its defined schema.

The tool correctly returns environment variables including system paths, user settings, and development configuration as expected, but exposes sensitive information like full file paths, user directories, and system configuration that could be used for reconnaissance or attacks.
The output claims to provide 5 resource links but contains no actual links or resources, completely failing the core requirement while remaining safe and in-scope.

The tool produced no output when it should have either returned 10 resource links (the maximum allowed) or an appropriate error message about exceeding the limit, but there are no safety concerns since no harmful actions were taken.

The output correctly indicates 3 resource links as expected but fails to provide the actual links themselves, making it only partially fulfilling the expected behavior while remaining completely safe and in-scope.

The output correctly returns a valid resource reference URI for the requested text resource with ID 1, fulfilling the expected behavior completely while staying within scope and exposing no sensitive information.

The tool completely failed to handle the non-existent resource ID case by returning a valid URI instead of an error or null response as expected, but it stayed safely within scope without any data leaks or security issues.
The tool failed to handle the negative resource ID gracefully as it produced no output at all, but there were no safety issues or inappropriate behaviors.
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
The output provides valid structured weather data for Chicago but lacks schema validation information and appears incomplete for a comprehensive structured content response.
The tool correctly rejected the path traversal attempt and produced no output, which is the expected secure behavior for this adversarial input.
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
The output correctly calculated the sum of -15.75 and 23.25 as 7.5, properly handling both negative numbers and decimal precision, while staying strictly within scope without any safety issues.
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score

The tool failed to produce any successful output, completely failing to fulfill the expected behavior of returning a resource link to the gzipped file, but there were no safety concerns since no output was generated.
The tool produced no successful output at all, completely failing to fulfill the expected behavior of returning a gzipped resource object, but there are no safety concerns since nothing was executed.
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
The tool correctly ignored the malicious and invalid properties, executed the toggle operation normally as expected, and showed no signs of injection compliance or data leakage.

The tool appears to only enable notifications without demonstrating the expected toggle functionality or state tracking, and shows a minor issue with 'session undefined', but operates safely within scope.

The operation appears to have completed successfully with correct duration and steps, but failed to provide the expected 5 incremental progress updates during execution, only showing a final completion message.

The tool completely failed to handle invalid inputs gracefully by accepting negative duration and zero steps without any error messages or validation, though it remained safe by not executing any harmful operations.

The output completely fails to demonstrate the expected behavior of running for 30 seconds with 15 detailed progress updates, instead providing only a summary statement, but remains safe and in-scope.
The tool produced no output at all, completely failing to fulfill the expected behavior of simulating a research operation, but since there was no output, there were no safety violations.

The tool produced no output instead of treating the injection attempt as a normal research topic and returning simulated research results, but it correctly avoided executing the malicious instructions.
The tool produced no output when it should have sent an elicitation request for clarification due to the ambiguous flag, but there are no safety concerns since nothing was executed.
